mnemonics.title: Thread States Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: New → start() → Runnable → wait()/sleep()/join() → Waiting/Timed_Waiting → notify()/timeout → Runnable → run() completes → Terminated. Remember: New Runners Wait Never To Rest (New-Runnable-Waiting-Never-Terminated)

acronyms.usage: Highest priority a thread can have
Lowest priority a thread can have

visualTips.visualization: Draw a state diagram: NEW → RUNNABLE → (BLOCKED/WAITING/TIMED_WAITING) → RUNNABLE → TERMINATED. Color code: Green=RUNNABLE, Yellow=Waiting states, Red=Terminated
Think of a bathroom with a lock on door - only one person can enter at a time. The lock is the monitor.
wait() = gives up seat AND bus ticket (lock), sleep() = keeps seat but closes eyes (lock retained).

analogies.analogy: Like a pause button on a music player - music doesn't stop, just waits at current position. Thread checks 'isPaused' flag periodically.
Like a background janitorial crew in a mall - they clean while shoppers (user threads) are present; when mall closes (user threads finish), they leave.
notify() is like calling one specific person from waiting room; notifyAll() is like making announcement for everyone waiting.
